channel.title: Object-Required on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Object-Required on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] VBA Object Required Error
item.description:
What This Error Means

Object Required (Error 424) occurs when VBA expects an object reference but receives a value instead. This happens when Set is omitted for object assignment or when a method is called on a Nothing object.

Common Causes

  • Missing Set keyword for object assignment
  • Object variable set to Nothing
  • Misspelled object name
  • Object was destroyed or closed
  • Using default property instead of object

How to Fix

' WRONG: Missing Set keyword
Dim ws As Worksheet
ws = Worksheets("Sheet1") ' Object Required

' CORRECT: Use Set for object assignment
Dim ws As Worksheet
Set ws = Worksheets("Sheet1")

' WRONG: Object is Nothing
Dim rng As Range
Set rng = Nothing
rng.Value = 1 ' Object Required

' CORRECT: Check for Nothing
If Not rng Is Nothing Then
 rng.Value = 1
End If

Examples

Sub Example()
 Dim wb As Workbook
 wb = Workbooks(1) ' Missing Set
 wb.Activate
End Sub

Related Errors

  • Object Variable Not Set - Nothing reference
  • Runtime Error - general execution errors

item.title: [Solution] VBA Runtime Error 424 Object Required Fix
item.description:
VBA Runtime Error 424: Object Required Fix

This error occurs when VBA encounters a variable or expression where an object is expected, but the value is Nothing or a simple data type. The full message is: Run-time error '424': Object required.

Description

In VBA, object variables must be set with the Set keyword. If you omit Set, or if an object variable is Nothing when you try to access its properties or methods, VBA raises error 424. This error also occurs when trying to use a non-object variable (such as a String or Long) as an object.
